subtasks.description: Generate SQL query to compute current month spend, 3-month rolling average, variance percentage, and flag anomalies. Return all rows with spend, 3-month average, variance %, and preliminary anomaly flag.

Reconcile SQL spend results with business thresholds and classification rules to classify each anomaly as normal_variance (<10%), concerning (10-25%), or critical (>25%). Include anomaly severity scoring and recommendation context.

plan.cost_rationale: Two subtasks: (1) sql_gen at mid tier to compute current vs. 3-month average with window functions (LAG/AVG OVER) across monthly granularity—moderately complex aggregation; (2) cross_recon at mid tier to apply classification thresholds and produce severity labels without needing external source reconciliation (the thresholds act as the second signal). Not frontier-level because the SQL doesn't require multi-CTE chaining and the classification is threshold-based, not inference-heavy. Not a policy_action because this is anomaly classification (posture/severity), not governance compliance. Minimum 2 subtasks because the output of spend computation must feed classification logic.

output.sql: WITH monthly_spend AS (SELECT date_trunc('month', event_date)::date AS month, SUM(amount_usd) AS spend FROM consumption_facts GROUP BY 1), rolling AS (SELECT month, spend, AVG(spend) OVER (ORDER BY month ROWS BETWEEN 3 PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING) AS avg_3mo FROM monthly_spend) SELECT month, spend, avg_3mo, CASE WHEN avg_3mo IS NULL OR avg_3mo = 0 THEN NULL ELSE ROUND(((spend - avg_3mo) / avg_3mo) * 100, 2) END AS variance_pct, CASE WHEN avg_3mo IS NULL OR avg_3mo = 0 THEN 'insufficient_data' WHEN ABS((spend - avg_3mo) / avg_3mo) * 100 < 20 THEN 'normal_variance' WHEN ABS((spend - avg_3mo) / avg_3mo) * 100 < 50 THEN 'concerning' ELSE 'critical' END AS anomaly_flag FROM rolling ORDER BY month
